channel.title: Information Technology on RaihnForge
channel.description: Recent content in Information Technology on RaihnForge

item.title: IT 202 — Change Ready: Technology Skills for Civic and Community Leaders
item.description:
Course Snapshot

  • Institution. Central Washington University.
  • Full title. Change Ready: Technology Skills for Civic and Community Leaders.
  • Catalog description. Develops practical productivity-software competency in Microsoft Word, Excel, Access, and PowerPoint, framed around the technology demands of civic and community leadership work. Paraphrased from the CWU course catalog.
  • Credits. 5
  • Prerequisites. None listed. The course is positioned as a general-education and ITAM-foundation offering open to students across majors.
  • Designation. Core requirement in the CWU Information Technology and Administrative Management program. The course also satisfies a general-education breadth requirement in several program plans.
  • Thematic frame. Office-suite literacy reframed as civic infrastructure. The course’s stated frame is that nonprofit boards, neighborhood associations, city advisory committees, and community organizations all run on the same handful of productivity tools, and competence in those tools is a precondition for effective civic participation.

Official Learning Outcomes

Paraphrased from CWU course-catalog and ITAM program materials. On completion, a student should be able to:

item.title: IT 248 — Web Fundamentals
item.description:
Course Snapshot

  • Institution. Central Washington University.
  • Full title. Web Fundamentals.
  • Catalog description. Introductory course in web development covering HTML structure, CSS styling, and the core practices of building accessible, standards-based web pages. Paraphrased from the CWU course catalog.
  • Credits. 4 to 5, depending on catalog year.
  • Prerequisites. None listed at the introductory level. Some sections recommend prior exposure to file systems and basic computing concepts from IT 101 or equivalent.
  • Designation. Core requirement in the CWU Information Technology and Administrative Management program. The course is the on-ramp for the web-focused upper-division IT and ITAM electives.
  • Thematic frame. Building for the open web. The course treats HTML and CSS as the two foundational languages of the public internet and trains students to write both by hand before any framework, content-management system, or builder tool enters the conversation.

Official Learning Outcomes

Paraphrased from CWU course-catalog and ITAM program materials. On completion, a student should be able to:

item.title: ITAM Project Management Internship Reflection
item.description:
1. Learning Objectives

My Spring 2025 internship at an EdTech employer focused on three core learning objectives that guided my development as a systems-oriented project manager and visual communicator. Building on foundational experiences from ITAM 490A, I sought to translate theory into practice by enhancing tool proficiency, streamlining operational workflows, and elevating cross-team communication through visual storytelling.

Objective 1 — Become Proficient with Jira

I deepened my knowledge of Jira by managing sprint cycles, grooming backlogs, and setting up project-specific dashboards. These systems were implemented across both the internal content-system product pipeline and the Upper Elementary microlesson content initiative. I structured Epics and Stories to reflect evolving production workflows, enabling greater task traceability and faster throughput. Dashboards became key tools in daily team syncs, reinforcing accountability and aligning output with strategic objectives.

item.title: Internship Week 03: Leveraging Technology
item.description:
1. Technology Usage

At the EdTech employer, technology plays a pivotal role in optimizing daily operations and enhancing workflow efficiency across teams. Three specific examples of tools and systems in use:

Jira (Atlassian)

Jira is the central tool for project tracking and task management. It enables our team to assign, prioritize, and monitor tasks within sprint cycles. This structure improves team coordination, reduces task redundancy, and increases transparency across cross-functional projects, especially during weekly sprint planning and retrospectives.

item.title: Final Paper: A Conceptual Map for Introduction to IT
item.description:
Introduction

Introducing Information Technology can seem daunting without an organized approach. It is a vast field filled with endless possibilities and challenges. While no one person can master all aspects of IT, a conceptual map will guide us through its infinite potential. Understanding IT requires a strong grasp of its foundations, insights into emerging technologies, an awareness of practical applications, and the ability to implement skills effectively in the IT profession.

item.title: Basics of Computer Hardware Components
item.description:
Having spent years using and modifying computers, I had developed an intrinsic understanding of two primary aspects of the machine: hardware and software. While the distinction was always evident, I had previously given little thought to the nuanced categories of system software and application software, perceiving them as generic terms. The nomenclature and taxonomy of these terms carry far more significance than I had understood.

System Software vs. Application Software

System software and application software are specific and mutually exclusive terms (Learn Computer Science, 2022). System software refers to programs that interact directly with computer hardware: operating systems, device drivers, and similar low-level tools. These often remain less visible to users because their interface and organizational focus is the machine itself.

item.title: ITAM Internship A Reflection
item.description:
1. Learning Objectives

My first learning objective was to gain experience as an Agile project-management scrum master for the development team. Though I assist in prioritization and help unblock the development team throughout sprints, our team does not ascribe to strict scrum implementation. I also prefer to avoid the scrum points system, as it creates a distraction and an alluring incentive for team members to prioritize the gamified system over the actual applied outcomes. My objective was modified to increase confidence and organization during developer daily standups and bi-weekly meetings. I incorporated weekly one-on-one meetings with individual developers in order to assess and assist with progress and prioritization.

item.title: Internship Week 05: Cloud as Sustainability Strategy
item.description:
1. Sustainability Initiatives at the Organization

Energy Reduction Through Cloud and Remote Work

The EdTech employer saves energy through two structural choices: cloud-vendor infrastructure and a fully remote workforce. By running our application on services like AWS and Heroku, we get secure, scalable servers that are partitioned from other tenants’ data but still co-located on shared physical hardware. That co-location is the point. Greater efficiency is gained when the server assets in use are kept as loaded and as well-maintained as possible, by experts whose entire job is keeping those machines efficient.

item.title: Internship Week 02: Contribution to the Company
item.description:
1. Contribution to the Company

As a project manager at the EdTech employer, I oversee SOC 2 Type 2 certification and lead a website redesign initiative. The SOC 2 Type 2 work is moving slowly but ramping up as we commit to audit dates in collaboration with our SOC 2 auditor. The website redesign has entered phase two. Phase one’s deliverable was confirming that the current site was up to date, compliant, and information-accurate. The redesign itself requires graphic-design collaboration with our design team, and because I have a graphic-design background I was chosen to execute it. The work is being done in Figma, a new tool for me, since past web designs only required Adobe Photoshop. I continue to work on these initiatives, though I have not yet been able to commit the time and attention I would prefer.

item.title: Can Closed Windows Be Open-Sourced?
item.description: Operating systems are no longer just helpful foundations for computers to be more functional. They are the digital landscapes many of us work, play, craft, and socialize inside on a daily basis. In my own case, working remotely with graphic design hobbies and keeping in touch with Veteran friends across the country, more of my time is spent in the OS environment than anywhere else. So what does it mean for individual privacy and liberty when this digital environment begins to aggressively exploit its user base’s attention, time, and personal data for profit? Windows 11 is the next iteration of the most prevalent operating system, and its implementation raises real concerns about individual privacy. What alternative solutions exist, and can they be viable against Microsoft’s goliath?

item.title: Atomic Transactions, Rights Matrices, and Recovery: A James River Jewelry Case
item.description:
1. Which Transactions Must Be Atomic?

The Acceptance Transaction and the Sales Transaction must be made atomic because their logical units of work (LUW) involve more than one table. Unintended results could occur if they are not declared as such. Once made atomic, this means that all changes within the transaction will be committed or none of them will. This introduces resource locking and prevents concurrency problems. The DBMS will automatically utilize implicit locks.

item.title: Spokeo v. Robins: Data Broker Privacy and Concrete Harm
item.description: Using Lessig’s four basic elements, Market, Law, Architecture, and Norms, this analysis seeks to better understand the regulatory effects and modality influences, both direct and indirect, that the case of Spokeo, Inc. v. Robins may have on personal liberty and ethics. The analysis reveals a potential critical disjoint in founding constitutional processes when addressing contemporary cybersecurity and privacy concerns. While the constitutional reasoning that allowed the Supreme Court to overturn the lower court’s initial finding in favor of Robins is clearly founded in precedent, it can be argued that “concrete evidence” of harm is not as easily obtained through the virtualization of persona and the datafication of people within cyberspace. Translation of ambiguous situations is required in order to accurately frame contemporary complexity.

item.title: Data Redundancy and First Normalization: IT 468 Assignment 1
item.description:
The first IT 468 assignment was a diagnostic on a single flat file that mixed project assignments with employee contact records. Four short questions asked us to find the redundancy problems, recommend storage changes to the name and phone columns, identify the data topics living together in the same table, and propose the files that should replace it. The answers below are mine as filed, lightly cleaned for presentation.

a) Serious Data Redundancy Problems

The PROJ_NAME field carries three unique project names duplicated across rows. PROJECT_NUM has the same shape of problem. Records and fields should be updated to unify the redundancies. All projects should be labeled in one record, and unique JOB codes for individuals should be stored on a different table. The deeper issue is that the topic of the table is too broad: it is asking one file to be the system of record for both projects and employees, and neither set of data ends up clean.

item.title: Lark Resorts Software Solutions Plan
item.description:
Lark Resorts is a fictional client built for the IT 260 final project, a Colorado-based hospitality company maintaining two properties in Vail and Telluride and preparing to expand into Alaska and California. The assignment was to write an executive summary that the company’s leadership could plausibly hand to a board, recommending a standardized software stack to support that expansion. What follows is that document.

The Mission

We must reconstruct our technology processes and applications in order to facilitate a cohesive, robust, responsible, and efficient infrastructure allowing for future growth and sustainment in our next organizational evolution.

item.title: Concerning Data: Ethics, Big Data, and the Three Groups Who Shape What We Believe
item.description: Data Science and Big Data are growing rapidly, and their impact on modern technology now operates on a global scale. From watching the news, to tracking viruses like COVID-19, to gathering information for our entertainment, data science and Big Data have a huge role in everyday life. The power of information impresses the necessity for ethical application. Producers, communicators, and audiences must be proactive in order to mature toward greater values and higher standards.

item.title: Manually Segmenting Data
item.description: I received a sales worksheet and was asked to organize it so the columns would actually support reporting and decision making. The exercise had two parts: group the fields into categories that match how the business uses them, and run a first pass of analysis that surfaces what the data can and cannot tell us. The memo below captures the segmentation, the pivot table conclusions, and the questions the dataset raised along the way.

item.title: Malware Analysis: Reading Obfuscated PowerShell Payloads
item.description: The assignment provided three captured command samples that looked like legitimate Windows operations on the surface and turned out to be malware payloads underneath. The exercise asked four questions of each sample: what is it trying to do, is it malicious, is it reaching out to another URL or IP, what other files is it trying to download, and how could a hacker make these commands harder to spot. The walkthrough below is the reasoning I worked out for each sample.

item.title: Stuxnet and the Olympic Games: The First Kinetic Cyberweapon
item.description:
Stuxnet and the Olympic Games

The malware used in the Stuxnet attack, known by its operational name Olympic Games, was a worm, “a type of malware that spreads copies of itself from computer to computer” (Norton, 2019). The worm is notable for many reasons, but the most consequential is that it was the first piece of malware engineered to cause kinetic destruction (Gibney, 2016). The Stuxnet virus has been likened to the achievement of nuclear weapons. While they are substantially different in form, they are similar in that each can cause a scale of degradation and destruction so enormous and catastrophic that it is difficult to predict.
